title: Atomic Withdrawal Transactions
description: The system shall ensure that withdrawal transactions are atomic, meaning all steps (deducting balance, updating limits, logging) either succeed together or fail entirely, with no partial changes allowed.
rationale: Ensuring atomicity prevents partial transactions which could lead to inconsistent data and potential financial discrepancies.
business_value: Maintaining data consistency is crucial for the trust and reliability of the financial system. This requirement protects users' funds from being partially withdrawn, thereby reducing risk and enhancing user confidence.

source.description: As a user I need withdrawal transactions to be atomic with full rollback if any part fails So that data remains consistent and no partial changes occur

acceptance_criteria: Given the user has a sufficient balance for a withdrawal
When the user initiates a withdrawal transaction
And a step in the transaction (e.g., logging) fails
Then the entire transaction is rolled back
And the user's balance is unchanged
And the withdrawal limits are unchanged
And no transaction log is recorded
preconditions: The user must have an existing account with sufficient funds
The system must be operational and available for transactions
postconditions: The user's balance remains unchanged after a failed withdrawal attempt
No changes are made to the withdrawal limits
Transaction logs are not recorded in case of failure
functional_constraints: All steps (deducting balance, updating limits, logging) must be executed atomically
If any step fails, all previous steps must be rolled back to their initial state

non_functional_requirements.performance: Transaction response time should be less than 5 seconds under normal load conditions
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support up to 1,000 transactions per minute without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should clearly communicate the failure reason and provide guidance on how to proceed

edge_cases: User has insufficient balance but tries to initiate a withdrawal
System is under high load and cannot handle additional transactions
Transaction log storage reaches its maximum capacity

interfaces.name: AccountService API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to deduct the user's balance.

interfaces.name: LimitService API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to update withdrawal limits.

interfaces.name: TransactionLog Database
interfaces.description: The requirement uses this database to record transaction logs and ensure they are rolled back on failure.

test_cases.description: User attempts withdrawal with sufficient balance but logging fails.
test_cases.expected_result: Transaction is rolled back, balance unchanged, limits unchanged, no log recorded.

test_cases.description: System under high load during withdrawal transaction initiation.
test_cases.expected_result: Transaction is rejected or delayed until system capacity increases, user is informed of delay/rejection reason.

test_cases.description: Database reaches its maximum capacity while recording transaction logs.
test_cases.expected_result: Transaction is rolled back, balance unchanged, limits unchanged, no log recorded. Error message indicating high load or maintenance is displayed to user.
